name: NeMo-Aligner
tagline: Scalable toolkit for aligning large language models with RLHF, DPO, and SteerLM
metaDescription: NeMo-Aligner is NVIDIA's toolkit for aligning large language models using RLHF, DPO, SteerLM, and SPIN, scaling across many GPUs via the NeMo Framework.
overview: NeMo-Aligner is a toolkit from NVIDIA for aligning large language models so they behave in a safer, more helpful way. It supports several alignment methods in one place, including supervised fine-tuning, reward model training, RLHF with PPO and REINFORCE, Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), SteerLM, and Self-Play Fine-Tuning (SPIN).

It is built on the NeMo Framework, so training can scale across many GPUs using tensor, data, and pipeline parallelism. This makes it suited to teams working with very large open models, such as the Nemotron and Llama 3 70B chat and reward variants that NVIDIA trained with it. Checkpoints stay compatible with the wider NeMo ecosystem for inference and further customization.

Note: as of May 15, 2025, the repository is no longer actively maintained. NVIDIA recommends NeMo RL (github.com/NVIDIA/NeMo-RL) for new post-training work. NeMo-Aligner remains useful as a reference and for reproducing existing NeMo-based alignment pipelines.
features: Multiple alignment algorithms in one toolkit: supervised fine-tuning, reward model training, RLHF (PPO and REINFORCE), DPO, SteerLM, and SPIN
SteerLM offers attribute-conditioned SFT as a user-steerable alternative to RLHF
Scales across thousands of GPUs using tensor, data, and pipeline parallelism via the NeMo Framework
Accelerated RLHF generation through TensorRT-LLM integration
Checkpoints are cross-compatible with the NeMo ecosystem for inference and further customization
Used to train released models including Nemotron-4-340B Instruct/Reward and Llama-3.1-Nemotron-70B-Instruct

gettingStarted.intro: NeMo-Aligner runs on machines with NVIDIA GPUs and is easiest to use through the prebuilt NeMo container, where it comes already installed.

steps.heading: Run the NeMo container
steps.body: On a machine with NVIDIA GPUs and drivers installed, start the NeMo container. NeMo-Aligner is already installed inside it under /opt/.
steps.code: docker run --gpus all -it --rm --shm-size=8g --ulimit memlock=-1 --ulimit stack=67108864 nvcr.io/nvidia/nemo:24.07

steps.heading: Or install into your own environment
steps.body: After installing the NeMo Toolkit (NeMo-Aligner shares its requirements plus PyTriton), add the aligner package with pip.

steps.heading: Install the latest commit instead
steps.body: If you prefer the newest source, clone the repo and install from the working directory.

steps.heading: Explore examples and tutorials
steps.body: Run alignment jobs using the GPT examples and follow the NeMo Framework alignment tutorials for end-to-end RLHF, DPO, and SteerLM workflows.

useCases: Run RLHF with PPO or REINFORCE to align a large open LLM to be safer and more helpful
Train a reward model from human preference data, such as the HelpSteer2 setup
Apply Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) as a simpler alternative to full RLHF
Use SteerLM to build a user-steerable model via attribute-conditioned fine-tuning
